file_name: GentechHoldingsInc_20190808_1-A_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_11776814_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_Distributor Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: Company desires to appoint Distributor as its exclusive distributor of the Products to all Clients that Distributor introduce, and upon confirmed the first order, this client come to be exclusive of Distributor, every new customer introduced by Distributor, must be approved by the company prior to any sale. After official approval from the Company, the customer introduced by Distributor, will appear on an official list in this contract, and will only be met by the Distributor and will follow the rules of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Company desires to appoint Distributor as its exclusive distributor of the Products to all Clients that Distributor introduce, however, every new potential client introduced by Distributor, requires no approval by the company prior to any sale. After been introduced by the Distributor, the potential client, will be able to purchase from Company or Distributor, at its own will.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires Company approval for each new client introduced by the Distributor. The changed text removes this requirement and also allow the client to purchase directly with the company, creating an in-text contradiction. This affects Section 1.a regarding exclusive appointments, since now the clients are not exclusively from the distributor, and can purchase directly with the company.
perturbation.contradicted_text: APPOINTMENT, ACCEPTANCE & SCOPE. (a) Exclusive Appointment. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Distributor Agreement, Company hereby appoints and grants Distributor the exclusive right to sell and distribute the Products to customers that Distributor introduce and confirmed by Company, (in all territory of United State of America (USA) and to render other services as a distributor for Company as set forth herein. Distributor shall limit its activities with respect to the Products to Customers located within the Territory and refrain from selling or otherwise transferring, directly or indirectly, the Products to any person outside the Territory, without the express written consent of Company. Company shall not sell or otherwise supply, directly or indirectly, the Products to any Distributor's Customer's, except by sale through the Distributor. The Company shall not contact any of Distributor's Customer's for any reason, without the prior written approval of Distributor. Source: GENTECH HOLDINGS, INC., 1-A, 8/8/2019
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text regarding the exclusivity of the distributor's rights. The original text and contradicted text state that the distributor has the exclusive right to sell to customers they introduce, subject to Company approval, and that the Company will not sell to the Distributor's Customers directly. The changed text eliminates the approval requirement and allows clients to purchase directly from the Company, violating the exclusive appointment granted in the contradicted text (Section 1.a). This creates a clear in-text contradiction and affects the enforceability of the exclusive appointment.

perturbation.original_text: Distributor will promptly inspect the Products upon receipt at Distributor's facility to determine whether any Products included in the shipment are in short supply, defective, or otherwise not in conformance with this Agreement. Within Seven (7) days of receipt of such Products, Distributor will notify Company of any shortages, defects, non-conformance, and Company will promptly replace such Products free of charge. Title to the Products shall pass to Distributor upon delivery.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor will inspect the Products upon receipt at Distributor's facility to determine whether any Products included in the shipment are in short supply, defective, or otherwise not in conformance with this Agreement. Within Thirty (30) days of receipt of such Products, Distributor will notify Company of any shortages, defects, non-conformance, and Company will replace such Products subject to Company's internal policy on returns and refunds. Title to the Products shall pass to Distributor upon full payment for such products.
perturbation.explanation: The original text enforces a 7-day notification period for any defects or shortages, and that Company will replace Products free of charge. The changed version extends this period to 30 days and says company will replace Products subject to Company's internal policy on returns and refunds, which might not mean "free of charge". Also the title is passed to Distributor upon delivery but the changed text says Title to the Products shall pass to Distributor upon full payment for such products. This contradiction creates uncertainty regarding the timeframe for reporting defects, the conditions under which replacements are provided, and when the ownership happens. This contradicts the spirit of Section 3(c), which warrants that products will be free from defects in design, materials and workmanship and conform with any specifications provided.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Company warrants and represents that the Products will be free from defects in design, materials and workmanship and conform with any specifications provided.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces multiple contradictions. First, it extends the notification period for defects from 7 to 30 days. Second, it changes the condition for product replacement from 'free of charge' to 'subject to Company's internal policy on returns and refunds'. Third, it alters when the title passes to the Distributor from 'upon delivery' to 'upon full payment'. These changes directly contradict section (c), which warrants that the products will be free from defects and conform to specifications. The potential for the company's return policy to require the distributor to pay for replacements undermines the original warranty, and the altered timing of title transfer directly conflicts with the original terms.

perturbation.original_text: Distributor is an independent contractor and is not and shall not be deemed to be an employee, legal representative, dealer, general agent, joint venture or partner of Company for any purpose. Distributor acknowledges that Company has not granted it any authority to make changes to Company's terms and conditions of sale, grant any warranties in excess of those extended by Company or limit its liabilities or remedies less than Company limits its liabilities and remedies, sign quotations, incur obligations (expressed or implied), or in general enter into contracts on behalf of Company or bind Company in any transaction with customers, governmental agencies or third parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor is an independent contractor and also is a general agent, with powers to enter into contracts on behalf of Company. Distributor acknowledges that Company has not granted it any authority to make changes to Company's terms and conditions of sale, grant any warranties in excess of those extended by Company or limit its liabilities or remedies less than Company limits its liabilities and remedies, sign quotations, incur obligations (expressed or implied), or in general enter into contracts on behalf of Company or bind Company in any transaction with customers, governmental agencies or third parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that the Distributor is an independent contractor and not a general agent, lacking the authority to bind the Company in any transaction. The changed text introduces an in-text contradiction by simultaneously stating that the Distributor is an independent contractor and also a general agent with powers to enter into contracts on behalf of Company. This directly contradicts the clause that immediately follows, where it states Distributor acknowledges Company has not granted it authority to enter into contracts on behalf of the company, creating confusion and potentially invalidating the independent contractor status. This also contradicts with Section 9 regarding indemnification and limitation of liability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: INDEMNIFICATION & LIMITATION OF LIABILITY. (a) Indemnification. Each Party ("Indemnifying Party") shall indemnify, hold harmless and defend the other Party ("Indemnified Party") and its officers, directors, agents, employees, and affiliates, from and against any and all claims, demands, actions, costs, expenses, liabilities, judgments, causes of action, proceedings, suits, losses and damages of any nature, which are threatened or brought against, or are suffered or incurred by, the Indemnified Party or any such person to the extent caused directly by acts or omissions of the Indemnifying Party relating to this Agreement, including without limitation (i) any negligent or tortious conduct, (ii) any breach of any of the representations, warranties, covenants or conditions of the Indemnifying Party contained in this Agreement, (iii) any violation of applicable laws or regulations, (iv) infringement or violation of any patent, copyright, trade secret, or other proprietary interest of any third party, and (v) any breach of any express or implied warranties relating to the Products, including implied warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose. (b) Limitation of Liability.IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER FOR ANY SPECIAL, INDIRECT, EXEMPLARY OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF THIS AGREEMENT OR PURCHASE OR USE OF THE PRODUCTS.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction. It states that the Distributor is both an independent contractor and a general agent with the power to enter into contracts on behalf of the Company. This directly contradicts the immediately following clause stating the Distributor lacks the authority to enter contracts, and conflicts with Section 9 regarding indemnification and limitation of liability, as a general agent would typically have greater liability than an independent contractor.
